signals.title: JARVIS operating repo
signals.detail: A private agent operating system with missions, tickets, worktrees, reports, Scribe, and Promoter.

signals.title: short memory, long repo
signals.detail: The thesis: chat is runtime exhaust; the repo is the durable product.

signals.title: Promoter
signals.detail: A publishing product that turns commits, reports, and lessons into reviewable public material.

signals.title: Proof rails
signals.detail: Nothing is done until there is a command, screenshot, report, commit hash, or live URL.

operatingModes.label: Builder
operatingModes.title: I turn fuzzy AI work into operating systems.
operatingModes.summary: The useful part is not prompting harder. It is giving the work durable shape: state files, proofs, handoffs, reports, and tools that can run again tomorrow.
proof: JARVIS Constitution turns chat into repeatable operating rules.
Worktrees make every agent task isolated and reversible.
Promoter packages proof into public material instead of opinions.

operatingModes.label: Operator
operatingModes.title: I care about systems that keep moving without heroics.
operatingModes.summary: The design bias is boring reliability with a sharp interface: small tickets, explicit dependencies, proof gates, dashboards, and scheduled audits.
proof: Night shift and satellites turn idle time into checked work.

operatingModes.label: Writer
operatingModes.title: The writing comes from the machinery, not a content calendar.
operatingModes.summary: The site should expose the ideas already proven by commits and reports. The voice stays direct, slightly skeptical, and grounded in what actually happened.
proof: Proof stories are generated from real repo evidence.
Promoter keeps drafts reviewable before anything goes public.

liveFeeds.title: Current build trace
liveFeeds.context: The personal site now reads like an operating profile: proof stories, source state, and a canvas map instead of a template portfolio.
liveFeeds.action: Turn the branch proof into a deployable public artifact.

liveFeeds.title: Continuity contract
liveFeeds.context: Every new session begins from repo files, not memory. That makes the human the editor while the system carries context.
liveFeeds.action: Expose the contract publicly without leaking private implementation details.

liveFeeds.title: Short memory thread
liveFeeds.context: The strongest public thesis is that short AI memory is not a chat problem; it is an operating-system design problem.
liveFeeds.action: Link the site, blog, and Promoter around that thesis.

liveFeeds.title: Public-safe proof
liveFeeds.context: The site needs proof without private URLs, employer detail, or invented metrics. It can show patterns, artifacts, and operating rules.
liveFeeds.action: Keep each proof story tied to a file, command, screenshot, or published post.

buildLog.title: The operating spine
buildLog.summary: Process, decisions, markdown tickets, generated graph, worktree lifecycle, and the first dashboard made the repo self-orienting.
buildLog.proof: M01-M05 completed with dashboard state, graph generation, worktree scripts, and daily/session reports.

buildLog.title: The machine room
buildLog.summary: Local model, router, night shift, and Hermes evaluation turned the repo from a plan into a running system.
buildLog.proof: M06-M09 produced local LLM routing, Task Scheduler night run, and a parked Hermes decision with findings.

buildLog.title: The public layer
buildLog.summary: Scribe, Promoter, the personal site, and the launch post turned private work into public proof.
buildLog.proof: Live post: https://words.balgaly.com/blog/short-memory-long-repo/ plus Promoter drafts, screenshots, and me.balgaly.com cockpit branch.

proofStories.title: The repo that survives the chat
proofStories.angle: AI work needs continuity, not longer context windows. The operating contract lives in files, not memory.
evidence: MASTERPLAN.md defines the mission sequence and acceptance demo.
MISSION_STATE.md holds current truth for every new session.
reports/sessions.log records proof lines instead of vibes.

proofStories.title: Worktrees as the unit of agent work
proofStories.angle: Main checkouts stay clean while short-lived worktrees carry isolated tasks from ticket to merge.
evidence: scripts/wt-new.ps1 creates ticket branches under C:/dev/.wt.
scripts/wt-done.ps1 merges, removes the worktree, and updates dashboard state.
Ticket dependencies are checked before a branch can start.

proofStories.title: Promoter turns proof into public material
proofStories.angle: The publishing workflow should prepare, rank, and package ideas while the human keeps editorial control.
evidence: Promoter generates LinkedIn and blog drafts from reports, commits, and lessons.
Each pack includes hooks, timing, image prompt, CTA, and checklist.
Archive controls keep posted and passed ideas out of tomorrow's queue.

proofStories.title: Scribe audits whether yesterday survived
proofStories.angle: A personal operating system needs an auditor that checks continuity, not another place to manually summarize work.
evidence: Scribe runs on a schedule and writes daily reports.
It scans local repos for fresh trace and stale assumptions.
Findings become dashboard state and backlog material.

systems.name: JARVIS
systems.role: Mission control for plans, tickets, worktrees, reports, and proof.

systems.name: Promoter
systems.role: Turns the proof trail into public writing and launch material.

systems.name: Scribe
systems.role: Audits whether daily work has durable trace and useful next actions.

systems.name: Worktree lifecycle
systems.role: Keeps agent development isolated from main checkouts.

systems.name: Router
systems.role: Sends cheap routine work to local models and keeps reasoning explicit.

writing.title: short memory, long repo.
writing.note: The public story of building a small operating system around an AI agent in three days.

writing.note: Why external memory is infrastructure, not a note-taking hobby.

writing.title: the context bar.
writing.note: A small UI idea for making agent work more legible.

writing.title: the package manager nobody asked for.
writing.note: Why AI capabilities need distribution and governance.

writing.title: things i didn't know.
writing.note: A longer note on learning by building systems that push back.
